segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: KAEDEN
segments.en: After that, you'll go to the sushi takeaway counter.
segments.zh: 之后，你去寿司外卖柜台。

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: Will I be preparing boxes of food?
segments.zh: 我要准备餐盒吗？

segments.speaker: KAEDEN
segments.en: For today, you'll just be helping the staff.
segments.zh: 今天你只需要帮忙打下手。

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: Yes, of course.
segments.zh: 好的，没问题。

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: Is that my only task on the sushi counter?
segments.zh: 我在寿司柜台就做这一件事吗？

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: Sure.
segments.zh: 当然。那很重要，对吧？

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: That's important, isn't it?

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: What should I do if there are any?
segments.zh: 如果有的话我该怎么办？

segments.speaker: KAEDEN
segments.en: Ask someone to put them in the dishwasher.
segments.zh: 找个人把它们放进洗碗机。

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: OK, thanks.
segments.zh: 好的，谢谢。我不想搞错什么。

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: I don't want to get anything wrong.

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的，还有别的吗？

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: Anything else?

segments.speaker: CHARLOTTE
segments.en: Understood.
segments.zh: 明白了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: My name's Liz Fuller and I'm a running coach with Compton Park Runners Club.
segments.zh: 我叫莉兹·富勒，是康普顿公园跑步俱乐部的跑步教练。欢迎收听我的播客。如果你正在考虑开始跑步——我来帮你。网上有很多训练计划，旨在帮助人们逐步达到跑5公里的水平。其中一些很棒，成千上万各个年龄段的人因此参加了全国各地的5公里比赛。人们喜欢它们，因为容易遵循，也不会逼得太紧。然而，它们并不适合所有人——尤其是如果你有心脏病或哮喘之类的问题，因为它们针对的是平均体能和跑步能力的人。另外，每个人都不一样——如果你有任何与自身需求相关的具体问题，也没有人能提供答案。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Welcome to my podcast.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If you're thinking about taking up running – I'm here to help.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are many training programmes available online which aim to help people build up to running 5 kilometres.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Some of them are great and thousands of people of all ages are taking part in 5-kilometre races across the country as a result.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: People like them because they're easy to follow and don't push them too hard.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: However, they don't work for everyone – especially if you suffer from something like a heart condition or asthma, because they're aimed at people with average fitness and running ability.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another thing is that everyone is different – and if you have any specific questions related to your needs, there's no one to provide any answers.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I have a couple of simple tips I always give to new runners.
segments.zh: 我有几个简单的建议，总是给新手跑者。我猜你被告知要跑得非常慢，直到体能提高——嗯，我发现那会阻碍进步。你应该以感觉舒适的速度跑，但给自己计时，并尝试每次跑得快一点。听音乐很有帮助——它能让你分心，帮助身体进入节奏。我觉得这比和朋友一起跑更好——尤其是大多数人都有竞争心，而这正是你刚开始时不需要的。我认为一天中的时间不是特别重要——有些人晚上状态更好，而另一些人是晨型人——但你需要保持一致，所以争取定期训练——开始时每周两次就够了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I expect you've been told to run very slowly until your fitness increases – well, I find that can prevent progress.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: You should run at a speed that feels comfortable, but time yourself and try to run a bit faster each time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Listening to music can be very helpful – it takes your mind off things and helps your body get into a rhythm.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I'd say that is better than running with a friend – especially as most people are competitive and that's not what you want when you're just starting.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I don't think the time of day is especially important – some people are better in the evening, while others are morning people – but you need to be consistent, so aim to train regularly – twice a week is enough to begin with.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: New members often say to me that they've been put off running either because they lack confidence, or they don't have time, or they think they dislike running.
segments.zh: 新成员经常对我说，他们不愿意跑步，要么是因为缺乏自信，要么是没有时间，要么是认为自己不喜欢跑步。比如，塞莉两年前在40岁时加入了俱乐部。她在学校时一直喜欢跑步，但不确定自己是否还能跑。她担心被落下，成为最慢的跑者。但她说，她感到非常受欢迎，很快就忘了这些担心。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Ceri, for example, joined the club two years ago at the age of 40.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: She'd always enjoyed running at school but wasn't sure if she'd be able to do it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: She was worried about being left behind and being the slowest runner.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But she says she was made to feel so welcome she soon forgot all about that.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: James had always hated the idea of running but a friend encouraged him to come along for a taster session and he hasn't looked back.
segments.zh: 詹姆斯一直很讨厌跑步这个想法，但一个朋友鼓励他来参加体验课，结果他就一发不可收拾了。尽管工作压力很大，他从不缺席训练。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: He never misses a training session despite having a really demanding job.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Leo was worried about having to commit himself to training sessions every week and wasn't sure he'd be able to fit training into his busy schedule.
segments.zh: 利奥担心每周都要参加训练，不确定自己能否在繁忙的日程中抽出时间。但在经历了工作上的巨大压力后，他来到我们这里尝试了一下。现在他说自己感觉放松多了，而且很期待每周的跑步。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But after experiencing a lot of stress at work he came along to us and gave it a go.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now he says he feels much more relaxed and he looks forward to his weekly run.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Mark is quite typical of our new members.
segments.zh: 马克是我们新会员的典型代表。他从不认为自己是个运动型的人，直到退休后才决定挑战自己，尝试跑5公里。他花了几个月才鼓起勇气联系我们，但一发现还有其他同龄人也刚开始跑步，他立刻就放心了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: He's never considered himself to be a sporty person and it was only when he retired that he decided to take up the challenge of trying to run 5 kilometres.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It took him months to find the courage to contact us but felt reassured immediately as there were other people his age who were only just taking up running for the first time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: My own journey hasn't been easy.
segments.zh: 我自己的经历也不容易。我37岁时跑了第一次马拉松，那时我已经有了两个孩子。我丈夫跑马拉松好多年了，但我从没想过会和他一起跑。我用了四个小时跑完了，但中途差点放弃——是观众的支持让我坚持了下来。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I did my first marathon when I was 37, after having had two kids.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: My husband had been running marathons for years, but I never dreamed I'd be doing one with him.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I managed to complete it in four hours, but I felt like giving up halfway through – it was only the support of the spectators that kept me going.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I do think signing up for a race of whatever length is motivating – whether it's 5K or 25K – because it's good to have something to work towards and it gives you a sense of achievement.
segments.zh: 我确实认为报名参加任何距离的比赛——不管是5公里还是25公里——都很有激励作用，因为有一个目标去努力是好事，而且能给你成就感。我六个月后就跑了第一个10公里，这当然很有挑战性，我不一定推荐这么做。但训练几周后，值得报名参加一个5公里——有些人发现他们只需要几次练习就能参赛，但我建议至少给自己几个月的时间。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I did my first 10K after only six months, which was certainly very challenging and not something I'd necessarily recommend.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But after you've been training for a few weeks, it's worth putting your name down for a 5K – some people find they only need a few practice runs before taking part in a race, but I'd give yourself a couple of months at least.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, I hope that's given . . .
segments.zh: 好了，我希望这些……

items.prompt: Which TWO problems with some training programmes for new runners does Liz mention?

pairs.note: 题干问问题，原文说'不适用于所有人'，是问题的同义表达。

pairs.q: some training programmes for new runners
pairs.p: training programmes available online which aim to help people build up to running 5 kilometres
pairs.note: 题干中的'训练项目'对应原文中的'training programmes'，'新跑者'对应'help people build up to running'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到原文中'However'后的内容，其中'don't work for everyone'直接对应题干中的'problems'。

pairs.note: 题干中的'training programmes'在原文中直接出现，帮助定位到相关内容。
原文直接提到'不适用于所有人'，对应题干中可能提到的缺点。

pairs.q: no one to provide any answers
pairs.p: no one to provide any answers
pairs.note: 原文指出'没有人提供答案'，对应题干中缺乏个性化指导的问题。

paraphrase.explain: 题干要求选择两个字母，但标准答案只给了一个E。定位句提到训练项目不适用于所有人，且没有人为具体问题提供答案，这些是缺点，对应选项E。

items.prompt: Which TWO tips does Liz recommend for new runners?

pairs.note: 题干中的tips与原文中的tips完全一致，直接定位到建议内容。
题干中的new runners与原文中的new runners完全一致，明确目标人群。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Liz给新跑者的建议，原文开头直接说'I have a couple of simple tips I always give to new runners'，因此定位到该句后，后续内容即为建议。

pairs.note: 题干中的'simple tips'在原文中直接出现，帮助定位建议内容。
题干中的'new runners'在原文中直接出现，明确建议对象。

paraphrase.explain: 题干要求选择两个字母，但标准答案只给了一个D，可能题目有误。定位句开头提到'have a couple of simple tips I always give to new runners'，直接对应题干中的'simple tips'和'new runners'，帮助定位到建议部分。

items.prompt: What reason prevented each of the following members of the Compton Park Runners Club from joining until recently?

pairs.note: prevented from joining 对应 been put off running，表示被阻止参加跑步
原文直接出现 lack confidence，与题干关键词完全一致

paraphrase.explain: 题干问阻止加入的原因，定位句开头提到新成员说他们被阻止跑步是因为缺乏自信、没时间或不喜欢跑步，其中缺乏自信是直接原因，对应选项A。

items.prompt: What reason prevented each of the following members of the Compton Park Runners Club from joining until recently?

pairs.q: reason prevented from joining until recently
pairs.p: had always hated the idea of running
pairs.note: 题干问阻止加入的原因，原文说一直讨厌跑步，这就是原因。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到James部分，原文说'had always hated the idea of running'对应题干中的reason prevented from joining。

items.prompt: What reason prevented each of the following members of the Compton Park Runners Club from joining until recently?

pairs.note: 题干问原因，原文用'worried about'表达顾虑，是原因的同义表达。

pairs.p: wasn't sure he'd be able to fit training into his busy schedule
pairs.note: 题干说'直到最近才加入'，原文解释之前因时间紧张未加入，是原因的具体描述。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到Leo部分，'worried about'和'busy schedule'直接对应题干问的阻止原因。

items.prompt: What reason prevented each of the following members of the Compton Park Runners Club from joining until recently?

pairs.p: never considered himself to be a sporty person
pairs.note: 题干问原因，原文用'never considered himself to be a sporty person'解释为何之前没加入。

pairs.p: only when he retired that he decided to take up the challenge
pairs.note: 题干说'直到最近才加入'，原文对应'直到退休才决定尝试'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到Mark的例子，题干问阻止他之前加入的原因，原文提到他从不认为自己运动型，直到退休才尝试，因此原因是他不认为自己运动型。

items.prompt: What does Liz say about running her first marathon?

pairs.note: 题干中的'first marathon'与原文'first marathon'完全一致，直接定位。

pairs.p: I managed to complete it in four hours, but I felt like giving up halfway through – it was only the support of the spectators that kept me going.
pairs.note: 题干问Liz对第一次马拉松的看法，原文这句话描述了她的感受和经历，是答案所在。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干关键词'first marathon'定位到原文'first marathon'，然后找到Liz描述感受的句子，其中提到'felt like giving up'和'support of the spectators'，对应选项C（她需要鼓励才能完成）。

items.prompt: Liz says new runners should sign up for a race

pairs.p: after you've been training for a few weeks
pairs.note: new runners 对应训练几周后的跑步者，即新手跑者

pairs.p: putting your name down for a 5K
pairs.note: sign up for a race 同义替换为 put your name down for a 5K，表示报名比赛

paraphrase.explain: 题干说新手应该报名比赛，定位句提到训练几周后值得报名5公里比赛，其中'new runners'被替换为'after you've been training for a few weeks'，'sign up for a race'被替换为'putting your name down for a 5K'，因此答案选B。
